---
title: "Key technologies used to build the plant package"
description: "Rich FitzJohn & Daniel Falster on the Rcpp/R6, Git, and continuous-integration stack behind plant. Archived pointer to the original MEE Methods blog post (23 Feb 2016) — opens the original."
date: 2016-02-23
author: "Rich FitzJohn & Daniel Falster"
categories: [announcement, external]
# Canonical source. This page is a local stub that redirects to it.
external-url: "https://methodsblog.com/2016/02/23/plant/"
reading-time: false
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link rel="canonical" href="https://methodsblog.com/2016/02/23/plant/">
        <meta http-equiv="refresh" content="0; url=https://methodsblog.com/2016/02/23/plant/">
---

::: {.eyebrow}
from the archive · external
:::

This post originally appeared on the
**[MEE Methods blog](https://methodsblog.com/2016/02/23/plant/)**
(*Methods in Ecology and Evolution*) on 23 February 2016, by
Rich FitzJohn and Daniel Falster. We keep a pointer to it here rather than
re-hosting it.

You're being redirected to the original. If that doesn't happen,
**[read it on the Methods blog →](https://methodsblog.com/2016/02/23/plant/)**

> It walks through the engineering behind `plant` — C++ wrapped with Rcpp
> and R6 classes, version control with Git/GitHub, and automated testing —
> the stack that lets the model be both fast and flexible.

It accompanies the package's methods paper, Falster *et al.*,
*Plant: A package for modelling forest trait ecology & evolution*,
*Methods in Ecology and Evolution*,
[doi:10.1111/2041-210X.12525](https://doi.org/10.1111/2041-210X.12525).

```{=html}
<script>window.location.replace("https://methodsblog.com/2016/02/23/plant/");</script>
```
